question: A company tracks average website sign-ups per year. Based on the chart, which of the following statements is true?

options.A: From this chart we cannot draw firm conclusions about how sign-ups will behave over the full latest year.
options.B: The latest year proves that sign-ups are now permanently declining.
options.C: The latest year shows a clear sharp drop in sign-ups compared with previous years.
options.D: The latest year has the lowest sign-ups of the entire period shown.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 5 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (634 sign-ups) directly to full-year values like 1,632 sign-ups creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
